title: Feature Flag System for Signal Dimensions
author: Cowork
problem: When the Signal Decay Monitor (or manual analysis) identifies a dimension as decayed or harmful, there is no clean way to disable it without a code change and redeploy. This creates risk: either a bad dimension keeps corrupting scores, or a hot-fix deployment introduces new bugs under pressure.
solution: A per-dimension boolean flag in config.py. Setting a flag to false zeroes that dimension's score and removes its direction vote from compute_confluence(). No code change required — only a config edit. Restoring the flag re-enables the dimension instantly.
scope: Tier 2 — Standard. Two-file change: config.py + signals.py. Fully backward-compatible (all flags default to true).
behaviour_when_disabled: Dimension score is 0 — does not contribute to total
No direction vote added to dim_directions — does not influence LONG/SHORT decision
Candlestick bonus is still applied (it is not a named dimension)
MTF gate (hard/soft penalty) is unaffected — it runs before dimension scoring
Disabled dimensions are logged at DEBUG level once per compute_confluence() call
use_cases: SOCIAL dimension shows consistent negative alpha during earnings season → disable it
REVERSION dimension fires false positives in trending bull market → disable it
NEWS dimension latency spikes due to upstream RSS issues → disable to reduce noise
Rapid A/B testing: disable one dimension per paper session, compare PnL
Signal Decay Monitor identifies dimension IC < 0.03 → flag it disabled in config
acceptance_criteria: Setting any flag to false produces score=0 for that dimension in unit tests
All flags false still returns a valid (score=0) result without exceptions
All flags true produces identical results to pre-flag behaviour
compute_confluence() result dict includes 'disabled_dimensions' key listing which were skipped
Tests pass in existing pytest suite
notes: Candlestick bonus is intentionally excluded from the flag system — it is a trade confirmation overlay, not a signal dimension. The MTF gate and candle gate are also excluded as they are filters, not scoring dimensions.
